report_name: CKR Consulting Engineers Hit by 'payload' Ransomware Group

threat_actors.name: payload
threat_actors.description: A ransomware group that employs double-extortion tactics. It listed CKR Consulting Engineers on its data leak site on July 19, 2026, indicating a successful network breach and data exfiltration.

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: The payload ransomware was deployed across the CKR Consulting Engineers network to encrypt files, rendering them inaccessible and causing significant business disruption.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over Web Service
attack_patterns.description: As part of the double-extortion model, the attackers exfiltrated sensitive data from CKR Consulting Engineers to attacker-controlled infrastructure before deploying ransomware, likely using encrypted web traffic.

attack_patterns.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter: PowerShell
attack_patterns.description: PowerShell was noted as a common tool for executing encoded commands or downloading files for lateral movement and payload delivery in ransomware attacks.

attack_patterns.name: Lateral Tool Transfer
attack_patterns.description: Tools such as PsExec and rclone are likely transferred to victim systems to facilitate lateral movement and data exfiltration, as suggested by hunting hints.

malware.name: payload ransomware
malware.description: The ransomware deployed by the 'payload' group to encrypt files and disrupt operations at CKR Consulting Engineers as part of a double-extortion attack.

tools.name: PsExec
tools.description: Suggested as a tool used for lateral movement in ransomware attacks of this type.

tools.name: rclone
tools.description: Suggested as a tool used for data exfiltration in ransomware attacks of this type.

identities.name: CKR Consulting Engineers
identities.description: A U.S.-based consulting and engineering firm that was breached by the 'payload' ransomware group and listed on their data leak site.

infrastructure.name: payload Ransomware Leak Site
infrastructure.description: The data leak site used by the 'payload' group to publicly list CKR Consulting Engineers as a victim and pressure them into paying a ransom.

infrastructure.name: payload Exfiltration Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: Attacker-controlled infrastructure used to receive sensitive data exfiltrated from CKR Consulting Engineers' network.

indicators.name: PowerShell Encoded Command Execution
indicators.pattern:
title: PowerShell Encoded Command Execution
id: 2b5c1f8a-3e4d-4b1a-8c9d-0f1e2d3c4b5a
status: experimental
description: Detects PowerShell processes executing encoded commands, a common tactic for obfuscating malicious scripts used in ransomware attacks.
references:
  - https://www.hookphish.com/blog/ransomware-group-payload-hits-ckr-consulting-engineers/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-20
tags:
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.execution
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\powershell.exe'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - ' -enc '
      - ' -encodedcommand '
      - ' -e '
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects PowerShell processes executing encoded commands, a common tactic for obfuscating malicious scripts used in ransomware attacks.

indicators.name: Potential Ransomware Tool Execution (PsExec, rclone)
indicators.pattern:
title: Potential Ransomware Tool Execution (PsExec, rclone)
id: 8d9e0f1e-2d3c-4b5a-a1b2-c3d4e5f6a7b8
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of PsExec or rclone, which are legitimate tools often abused by ransomware groups for lateral movement and data exfiltration, respectively.
references:
  - https://www.hookphish.com/blog/ransomware-group-payload-hits-ckr-consulting-engineers/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-20
tags:
  - attack.t1570
  - attack.t1021.002
  - attack.t1567
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\PsExec.exe'
      - '\rclone.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of PsExec or rclone, which are legitimate tools often abused by ransomware groups for lateral movement and data exfiltration, respectively.

indicators.name: Known Ransomware Utility Filenames
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Known Ransomware Utility File Creation
id: 5a6b7c8d-9e0f-4a1b-8c9d-0f1e2d3c4b5a
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation of files named PsExec.exe or rclone.exe, which are commonly used in ransomware attacks.
references:
  - https://www.hookphish.com/blog/ransomware-group-payload-hits-ckr-consulting-engineers/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-20
tags:
  - attack.t1570
level: medium
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith:
      - '\PsExec.exe'
      - '\rclone.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects files named PsExec.exe or rclone.exe, which are commonly used in ransomware attacks.
